extracted_results.title: HOXA9 and β-catenin safeguard HSC integrity.
key_findings: HOXA9 and β-catenin maintain HSC integrity
their cooperative interaction is crucial for HSC function
dysregulation leads to HSC exhaustion
extracted_results.methods: N/A
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it provides insights into the molecular mechanisms that regulate hematopoietic stem cell integrity.

extracted_results.title: Role of HOXA9 in solid tumors: mechanistic insights and therapeutic potential.
key_findings: HOXA9 functions as a transcription factor
aberrant expression of HOXA9 is associated with prognosis in solid tumors
signaling pathways and post-transcriptional regulation of HOXA9 are crucial in solid tumor progression
extracted_results.methods: Review of existing literature
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it provides insights into the role of HOXA9 in solid tumors, which could lead to the development of new therapeutic strategies.

extracted_results.title: HOXA9 transcription factor is a double-edged sword: from development to cancer progression.
key_findings: HOXA9 serves as a molecular orchestrator in cancer stemness and metastasis
HOXA9 has a dual role as an oncogene or tumor suppressor
HOXA9 is involved in fundamental biological processes such as embryogenesis and tissue homeostasis
extracted_results.methods: N/A
extracted_results.limitations: The multiple modes of regulation and context-dependent interactions of HOXA9 are not fully understood, making it challenging to design targeted therapies
extracted_results.relevance: Understanding the role of HOXA9 in cancer progression and development is crucial for designing effective targeted therapies and developing personalized treatment strategies.

extracted_results.title: HOXA9 Regulome and Pharmacological Interventions in Leukemia.
extracted_results.journal: Advances in experimental medicine and biology
key_findings: HOXA9 is a strong indicator of poor prognosis in leukemia patients
HOXA9 expression correlates with patient survival outcomes and response to therapy
HOXA9 regulome provides new therapeutic opportunities
extracted_results.methods: N/A
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it provides new insights into the biochemical mechanisms underlying HOXA9-mediated leukemogenesis and potential pharmacological approaches to treat HOXA9-driven leukemia.

extracted_results.title: A Menin-MLL Inhibitor Induces Specific Chromatin Changes and Eradicates Disease in Models of MLL-Rearranged Leukemia.
key_findings: VTP50469 inhibits Menin-MLL interaction
VTP50469 induces chromatin changes and apoptosis in MLL-r leukemia cells
VTP50469 eradicates disease in MLL-r leukemia models
extracted_results.methods: Structure-based design, cell lines, patient-derived xenograft models
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it presents a potential therapeutic strategy for MLL-rearranged leukemia, a type of blood cancer.

extracted_results.title: HOXA9 promotes proliferation, metastasis and prevents apoptosis in hepatocellular carcinoma.
extracted_results.journal: Journal of cancer research and clinical oncology
key_findings: HOXA9 is upregulated in HCC and predicts poor survival
HOXA9 promotes proliferation, metastasis, and prevents apoptosis in HCC
HOXA9 is regulated by Ribosomal protein RPL38 in HCC
extracted_results.methods: Bioinformatic analysis, qPCR, Western blot, in vitro cell assays, RNA sequencing, and xenograft tumor model
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it identifies HOXA9 as a potential therapeutic target for the treatment of hepatocellular carcinoma.

extracted_results.title: Menin Inhibition With Revumenib for KMT2A-Rearranged Relapsed or Refractory Acute Leukemia (AUGMENT-101).
extracted_results.journal: Journal of clinical oncology : official journal of the American Society of Clinical Oncology
key_findings: Revumenib showed promising efficacy and safety in patients with KMT2A-rearranged acute leukemia
The CR + CRh rate was 22.8%, exceeding the null hypothesis of 10%
Overall response rate was 63.2% with 68.2% of patients having no detectable residual disease
extracted_results.methods: Phase I/II, open-label, dose-escalation and expansion study of revumenib
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it represents the largest evaluation of a targeted therapy for patients with KMT2A-rearranged relapsed or refractory acute leukemia.

extracted_results.title: Comparison of Multiple Clinical Testing Modalities for Assessment of NPM1-Mutant AML.
key_findings: RT-PCR showed superior sensitivity compared to NGS, IHC, and FC for detecting mutant NPM1
NGS, IHC, and FC had positive predictive values of ≥ 80%, indicating positive results are typically associated with RT-PCR positivity
FC did not identify any NPM1-mutated residual disease not already detected by RT-PCR, NGS, or IHC
extracted_results.methods: Peripheral blood and bone marrow samples were tested by quantitative, real-time, RT-PCR, NGS, IHC, and FC
extracted_results.limitations: The main challenge of NGS, IHC, and FC was identifying low disease burden (<0.5% NCN by RT-PCR)
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it informs future clinical testing algorithms for assessing NPM1-mutant AML and detecting residual disease.

extracted_results.title: Epigenetic regulation of noncanonical menin targets modulates menin inhibitor response in acute myeloid leukemia.
key_findings: Menin inhibitors can be resisted by noncanonical menin targets
Loss of PRC1.1 subunits leads to menin inhibitor resistance
Combinational treatment with venetoclax overcomes resistance to menin inhibition
extracted_results.methods: Systematic chromatin-focused CRISPR screens, genetic, epigenetic, and pharmacologic studies
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it provides potential strategies to treat acute myeloid leukemia with compromised PRC1.1 function, which can help overcome resistance to menin inhibitors.

extracted_results.title: Acute myeloid leukemia management and research in 2025.
extracted_results.journal: CA: a cancer journal for clinicians
key_findings: 12 new agents have received regulatory approval for AML in the United States since 2017
menin inhibitors and CD123 antibody-drug conjugates show promising results
oral decitabine-cedazuridine can be used as a bioequivalent alternative to parenteral hypomethylating agents in AML
extracted_results.methods: N/A
extracted_results.relevance: This research is relevant because it highlights recent advances in acute myeloid leukemia management and identifies promising areas for future research.

extracted_results.title: Therapeutic implications of menin inhibition in acute leukemias.
key_findings: Menin inhibitors show promise in treating acute leukemias with KMT2A rearrangement
Menin inhibition may also target leukemias with mutated NPM1
Early clinical data validates the proof-of-concept for menin inhibition in susceptible leukemia subsets
extracted_results.methods: Rationally designed clinical studies investigating small-molecule oral menin inhibitors
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it explores a novel therapeutic approach for acute leukemias with poor outcomes using current standard therapies.

extracted_results.title: O-GlcNAc-modified HOXA9 suppresses ferroptosis via promoting UBR5-mediated SIRT6 degradation in nasopharyngeal carcinoma.
key_findings: HOXA9 is highly expressed in NPC and promotes tumor growth
O-GlcNAcylation stabilizes HOXA9 and facilitates its nuclear translocation
HOXA9 inhibits ferroptosis by enhancing UBR5 expression and SIRT6 degradation
extracted_results.methods: GEO dataset analysis, cell viability assays, immunoprecipitation, ChIP and luciferase assays, Co-IP assays, and subcutaneous NPC mouse model
extracted_results.relevance: This study provides potential therapeutic targets for nasopharyngeal carcinoma treatment by elucidating the regulatory mechanism of ferroptosis.

extracted_results.title: Preclinical efficacy of the potent, selective menin-KMT2A inhibitor JNJ-75276617 (bleximenib) in KMT2A- and NPM1-altered leukemias.
key_findings: JNJ-75276617 inhibits menin-KMT2A interaction, reducing target gene expression
JNJ-75276617 shows potent antiproliferative activity in AML and ALL cell lines and patient samples
JNJ-75276617 exhibits synergistic effects with other therapies and overcomes resistance to revumenib
extracted_results.methods: In vitro and in vivo experiments, including cell line studies, patient sample analysis, and xenograft models
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it presents a potential new therapeutic option for acute leukemias with KMT2A or NPM1 alterations.

extracted_results.title: HOXA9 versus HOXB9; particular focus on their controversial role in tumor pathogenesis.
key_findings: HOXA9 and HOXB9 have distinctive functions in tumor advancement
HOX genes are essential for regulating cellular processes
Aberrant functioning of HOX genes is linked to many diseases, especially cancer
extracted_results.methods: N/A
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it explores the roles of HOXA9 and HOXB9 in tumor pathogenesis, which can lead to a better understanding of cancer development and progression.

extracted_results.title: HOXA9-methylated DNA as a diagnostic biomarker of ovarian malignancy.
key_findings: Meth-HOXA9 was detected in 93% of ovarian cancer specimens
Meth-HOXA9 had a diagnostic accuracy of 98%
Meth-HOXA9 was detected in 17% of benign ovarian tumors
extracted_results.methods: Droplet digital PCR
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it identifies meth-HOXA9 as a potential diagnostic biomarker for ovarian malignancy.

extracted_results.title: TP53 Inactivation Confers Resistance to the Menin Inhibitor Revumenib in Acute Myeloid Leukemia.
extracted_results.journal: bioRxiv : the preprint server for biology
key_findings: TP53 mutations are associated with de novo resistance to revumenib
TP53 mutations impair induction of TP53 transcriptional targets
MCL-1 inhibitor MIK665 sensitizes TP53-mutant AML cells to revumenib
extracted_results.methods: Analysis of a panel of cell lines and an isogenic model
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it identifies a potential biomarker for resistance to a promising treatment for acute myeloid leukemia.

extracted_results.title: Effective Menin inhibitor-based combinations against AML with MLL rearrangement or NPM1 mutation (NPM1c).
key_findings: Menin inhibitor treatment disrupts the interaction between Menin and MLL1 or MLL1-fusion protein
Co-treatment with Menin inhibitor and BCL2 or CDK6 inhibitors induces synergistic lethality in AML cells
Combined therapy with Menin inhibitor and venetoclax exerts superior in vivo efficacy in AML xenografts
extracted_results.methods: Pre-clinical studies using genetic knockout, degradation of Menin, and treatment with Menin inhibitors
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it highlights the promise of Menin inhibitor-based combinations as a potential treatment for Acute Myeloid Leukemia (AML) with MLL rearrangement or NPM1 mutation.

extracted_results.title: MEN1 mutations mediate clinical resistance to menin inhibition.
key_findings: MEN1 mutations mediate clinical resistance to menin inhibition
inhibitor-menin interface mutations represent a conserved mechanism of therapeutic resistance
these mutants attenuate drug-target binding and prevent inhibitor-induced eviction of menin and MLL1 from chromatin
extracted_results.methods: phase 1 first-in-human clinical trial, xenograft models, and unbiased base-editor screen
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it demonstrates a common mechanism of therapeutic resistance to a chromatin-targeting therapeutic drug, which has implications for the development of effective treatments for leukaemia.

key_findings: HOXA9 is associated with leukemia and poor prognosis in AML
HOXA9 expression is linked to aggressiveness in AML
HOXA9 can be targeted indirectly or directly for cancer treatment
extracted_results.methods: Review of existing research on HOXA9 targeting
extracted_results.limitations: No specific limitations mentioned in the abstract
extracted_results.relevance: This research is relevant because it explores potential therapeutic strategies for targeting HOXA9 in acute myeloid leukemia and other cancers.

extracted_results.title: HOXA9 and CD163 potentiate pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma progression.
key_findings: HOXA9 and CD163 are highly expressed in PDAC and associated with poor prognostic indicators
High HOXA9 and CD163 expression are correlated with worse overall survival
CD163 is an independent prognostic marker of worse survival

extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it identifies HOXA9 and CD163 as potential therapeutic targets and prognostic markers for pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma.

extracted_results.title: Functions of the native NPM1 protein and its leukemic mutant.
key_findings: NPM1 protein acts as histone chaperone and shuttles between nucleus and cytoplasm
NPM1 is mutated in 30-35% of adult acute myeloid leukemia (AML)
NPM1 mutant delocalizes to cytoplasm in leukemic cells
extracted_results.methods: Review of existing research and murine models of NPM1 mutations
extracted_results.relevance: This research is relevant because it helps understand the functions of the NPM1 protein and its leukemic mutant, which is crucial for developing treatments for acute myeloid leukemia (AML).

extracted_results.title: The menin inhibitor revumenib in KMT2A-rearranged or NPM1-mutant leukaemia.
key_findings: 30% rate of complete remission or complete remission with partial haematologic recovery
low frequency of grade 3 or higher treatment-related adverse events
remissions occurred in leukaemias refractory to multiple previous lines of therapy
extracted_results.methods: first-in-human phase 1 clinical trial investigating revumenib in patients with relapsed or refractory acute leukaemia
extracted_results.limitations: asymptomatic prolongation of the QT interval on electrocardiography was identified as the only dose-limiting toxicity
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it establishes menin inhibition as a therapeutic strategy for susceptible acute leukaemia subtypes.

extracted_results.title: Mutant NPM1 Maintains the Leukemic State through HOX Expression.
key_findings: Loss of NPM1c results in downregulation of HOX genes and differentiation
NPM1c is required to maintain the leukemic state
XPO1 inhibition relocalizes NPM1c and promotes differentiation
extracted_results.methods: Experimental methods including nuclear relocalization and targeted degradation of NPM1c, and XPO1 inhibition
extracted_results.relevance: This study is relevant because it provides a rationale for the use of nuclear export inhibitors in acute myeloid leukemia with mutated NPM1.

extracted_results.title: Acute myeloid leukemias with UBTF tandem duplications are sensitive to menin inhibitors.

UBTF-TD protein maintains genomic occupancy at ribosomal DNA loci and occupies genomic targets commonly dysregulated in UBTF-TD myeloid malignancies
SNDX-5613 menin inhibitor reduces tumor growth and abrogates the UBTF-TD leukemic expression signature
extracted_results.methods: genomic occupancy analysis, protein degradation system, in vitro and in vivo tumor growth assays
extracted_results.relevance: This study provides a viable therapeutic strategy for patients with high-risk acute myeloid leukemia subtype characterized by UBTF tandem duplications.
